name: sshx
description: Fast, collaborative live terminal sharing over the web
readme_summary: sshx is a Rust-based tool for secure, collaborative live terminal sharing over the web. It allows multiple users to interact with a shared terminal session in real-time, featuring end-to-end encryption and a globally distributed server network for low latency.
key_features: Run a single command to share your terminal
Resize, move windows, and freely zoom and pan on an infinite canvas
See other people's cursors moving in real time
Connect to the nearest server in a globally distributed mesh
End-to-end encryption with Argon2 and AES
Automatic reconnection and real-time latency estimates
Predictive echo for faster local editing
primary_use_case: Collaborative live terminal sharing for remote work, debugging, and pair programming.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized illustration of a shared digital workbench where multiple hands, represented by glowing digital avatars, are simultaneously manipulating a single, complex 3D blueprint of a terminal interface. The blueprint is being actively edited, with cursors and lines of code appearing and disappearing in real-time. Subtle data streams flow between the avatars and the blueprint, symbolizing collaboration and instant feedback. The background is a clean, abstract digital space with subtle network nodes. The overall mood is dynamic and collaborative, highlighting seamless real-time interaction. The style should be a modern 3D isometric illustration with bright, engaging colors and clear visual cues of shared activity.

name: meshgen
description: Use AI Agents directly in Blender.
readme_summary: MeshGen is a Blender add-on that allows users to control Blender functionalities using natural language commands interpreted by AI agents. It supports various local and remote AI model backends for inference, enhancing creative workflows by integrating AI directly into the 3D modeling process.
key_features: AI agent control of Blender via natural language
Multiple backend options: local (llama.cpp, Ollama), remote (Hugging Face, Anthropic, OpenAI)
Optional LLaMA-Mesh integration for local mesh understanding
Optional Hyper3D integration for high-fidelity mesh generation
primary_use_case: Enabling users to control and manipulate 3D models and scenes within Blender using natural language commands processed by AI agents.
cover_image_prompt: A 3D isometric illustration of a sculptor in a bustling digital workshop, holding a glowing chisel. Instead of traditional tools, the sculptor is speaking commands into a sleek, futuristic microphone. The microphone's sound waves transform into intricate 3D mesh structures that materialize on a digital workbench. Small, subtle UI elements displaying code snippets and AI agent icons float around the scene, indicating the AI's role in interpreting the spoken words and generating the complex geometry. The overall mood is creative and futuristic, with a clean, organized composition and a vibrant color palette highlighting the transformation from voice to form. The repository name 'MeshGen' is subtly integrated into a tool's label.

name: claudecode-telegram
readme_summary: claudecode-telegram is a Python-based bridge that connects Telegram to Claude Code, allowing users to interact with Claude Code by sending messages through a Telegram bot. It leverages tmux and Cloudflare Tunnel to facilitate this communication, enabling responses to be sent back to Telegram.
key_features: Telegram bot integration for Claude Code
Message injection into Claude Code via tmux
Response retrieval from Claude Code's transcript
Setup via Cloudflare Tunnel for external access
Bot commands for status, clearing, resuming, and looping
primary_use_case: To enable users to interact with and receive responses from Claude Code through a familiar Telegram interface.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic robot sitting at a desk, holding a Telegram messaging bubble in one hand and a glowing code editor window in the other. The robot is actively typing a message into the code editor, which is visually represented as a stream of digital particles flowing from the Telegram bubble. On a small screen next to the robot, a snippet of the repository's name 'claudecode-telegram' is subtly displayed. The background is a clean, modern workspace with soft, ambient lighting, suggesting a seamless connection between communication and coding. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, inviting color palette, emphasizing ease of use and connectivity.

name: ProcessHacker
description: A free, powerful, multi-purpose tool that helps you monitor system resources, debug software and detect malware—mirror of https://github.com/processhacker2/processhacker.git
readme_summary: System Informer is a free, open-source tool for Windows that provides detailed system monitoring, software debugging, and malware detection capabilities. It offers real-time insights into system activity, resource usage, network connections, and file access, allowing users to manage processes and services effectively.
key_features: Detailed system activity overview with highlighting
Graphs and statistics for resource hog tracking
File usage detection
Network connection monitoring
Real-time disk access information
Detailed stack traces with kernel-mode, WOW64 and .NET support
Service creation, editing, and control
Portable and no installation required
primary_use_case: Monitoring system resources, debugging software, and detecting malware on Windows.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant, futuristic librarian meticulously organizing a vast, glowing library of information. Each book represents a running process or system resource. The librarian uses a magnifying glass that reveals hidden details and connections within the books, highlighting any anomalies or potential threats in red. Successfully managed resources are neatly shelved, emitting a soft, organized glow. Small, holographic screens around the librarian display real-time data streams and system statistics. The scene is set in a clean, modern digital space with a cool, analytical color palette. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and detail, with the 'System Informer' name subtly integrated into the library's architecture.
